title: Alignment
pickOne: **Good** - Endanger yourself to combat an unnatural threat.
**Neutral** - Teach someone the ways of your people.
**Chaotic** - Eschew a convention of the civilized world.

title: Background
description: Choose your background.

title: Beast-Blooded
description: Your heritage includes the blood of a beast or a monster. Describe your species, appearance, and cultural heritage, choosing anything or any combination of things that sounds interesting to you. Choose up to three **heritage moves** to start with, based on the monster moves that best match your heritage. Then choose a method of how you **honor your heritage**:
pick: Guarding or purifying the natural world
Enforcing your people's code of honor
Venerating your ancestors or ancestral deity

submoves.title: Appreciation
submoves.description: When you gain a new appreciation for your heritage, add a new **heritage move** or change an existing one, up to a maximum of three.

title: Blood and Tradition (+CON)
description: At the beginning of a session, or when you **honor your heritage**, roll+CON.

outcomes.text: Hold 2, in addition to whatever the GM says.

submoves.description: Spend a hold to make a **heritage move**; no other roll is necessary, you just make that move.

title: Fight Like a Beast
description: Your horns, fangs, claws, or talons are lethal weapons with a range of hand.

title: King of the Jungle (+WIS)
description: You can speak with and understand animals, and even closely study them and Parley with them as if they were people. When you **exert your will over a wild animal**, roll+WIS.

outcomes.text: The animal will perform one simple task for you before departing, to the best of its ability.
The animal will either run away or attack you, your choice.

title: Eye of the Tiger
description: When you **mark an animal** (with mud, dirt, or blood), you can see through that animal's eyes as if they were your own, no matter what distance separates you. Only one animal at a time may be marked in this way.

title: Fair Form
description: With a bit of a disguise, such as a hooded cloak, you are able to hide your heritage and pass as a member of another race, as long as you don't use your **heritage moves** or draw attention to yourself.

title: Forager
description: When you **Make Camp** in the wilderness, you and one other person don't need to consume a ration.

title: Night-Eyes
description: You can see clearly in the faintest of light; even starlight is enough for you.

title: Pack Leader
description: Choose a second way you can **honor your heritage**.

title: Pathfinder (+WIS)
description: You know places in the wilderness where secret paths lie unseen. When you **travel by a hidden path through the wilderness**, roll+WIS.

outcomes.text: It leads you where you want.
It leads you where you want, but choose a difficulty:
bullets: Others find the way who did not know it before
The journey takes much longer than it seemed to
The way is long and hard; each person who takes it must mark the debility weak
You encounter some danger upon the road

title: Predatory Instinct
description: When you use a **heritage move**, take +1d4 damage forward or +2 armor forward.

title: Savage Beast
description: Gain a non-multiclass move from the barbarian or ranger playbooks.

title: Sharp Senses
description: When you **Discern Realities** about a location, add these questions to the list:

bullets: How could I be accepted by the people here?
How could I best attract attention here?
What here is a source of evil?
Where could I best hide around here?

title: Tarzan
description: When you use a **heritage move** to evade harm or avoid an obstacle, you may also bring someone else along with you safely.

title: Akela (Requires level 6+)
description:
Requires: **King of the Jungle**

When you use **King of the Jungle**, on a 12+ the animal will either tag along with you and assist you for a while, in its own way, or you may command it to perform a very dangerous task for you.

title: Ancient Heritage (Requires level 6+)
description: You may now have up to five **heritage moves**.

title: Awakened Blood (Requires level 6+)
description: Choose another type of creature that has contributed to your heritage. When you change your **heritage moves**, you may take moves from this new heritage, if you wish. When you take this move, you may change one of your existing **heritage moves** to a move from your new heritage.

title: Bloody Roar (Requires level 6+)
description: While you are **afflicted by fear, mind control, or enchanting magic**, you may ignore the effect as long as you attack the closest enemy in sight. If you have no enemies in sight, the GM chooses who you attack.

title: Dire Beast (Requires level 6+)
description: Gain a non-multiclass move from the barbarian or ranger playbooks.

title: I Am the Tiger (Requires level 6+)
description:
Requires: **Eye of the Tiger**

When you **touch an animal and push your thoughts into its mind**, roll+WIS.

outcomes.text: You can read the animal's mind and memories, reliving its past and seeing what it has seen.
As above, but you have trouble separating these memories from your own, and you exhibit some aspects of the animal's behavior for a while after you break the link.

title: Killer Instinct (Requires level 6+)
description:
Replaces: **Predatory Instinct**

When you use a **heritage move**, take +1d8 damage forward or +4 armor forward.

title: Monster Blood (Requires level 6+)
description: When you roll for **Blood and Tradition**, you get +1 hold, even on a 6-.

title: Natural Medicine (Requires level 6+)
description:
Requires: **Forager**

When you **have time to gather supplies in the wilderness**, you can prepare a poultice that will heal one debility next time you **Make Camp**.

title: Nose For Trouble (Requires level 6+)
description: When **something hidden threatens you**, the GM will give you a warning in the form of a hunch about the nature of the danger, but not necessarily where it lurks.
